component.name: JSignPdf
component.summary: Add digital signatures to PDF documents

description.p: JSignPdf is a free, open source application that adds digital signatures to PDF documents. It supports visible and hidden signatures, certificate levels, timestamping, and batch processing from the command line.
Patch release fixing the Linux Flatpak build for the 3.0.0 release.

ul.li: Fix: drop the stale conf/ reference from the Flatpak manifests

description.p: Major release with a brand-new desktop UI and modernized packaging.

ul.li: New JavaFX GUI with PDF preview, drag-to-place visible signatures, and drag-and-drop loading; legacy Swing UI remains available via -Djsignpdf.swing=true
New Windows installers built with jpackage (EXE, MSI, portable ZIP with bundled JRE)
New Linux Flatpak bundle with bundled OpenJDK 21
Java 21 is now the minimum runtime
Named signature-setting presets — save and switch between signing configurations
Configuration moved to XDG-aware locations; legacy ~/.JSignPdf settings migrate automatically
Read passwords from stdin (opt in with --enable-stdin-passwords)
Migrated PDF engine to OpenPDF 3; updated PDFBox to 3.0.7 and BouncyCastle to 1.84
Improved symlink handling on macOS; UTF-8 translation bundles with many language updates
